# v13.1 release notes: skill-driven, memory-mined, multi-iteration

## Problem scope

Release notes at this project's bar are a **narrated release**, not a changelog dump — and the v13.1 window is large: 663 merged PRs / 748 closed issues ticketed (cutoff `2026-06-12`, v13.0.0 @ `92eb323fb`), project board 13 carrying 160 items (19 todo / 2 in progress / 139 done), and an estimated **300–500 changes not yet filed as tickets at all** — the ticket record lags the shipped reality, so no single enumeration source tells the story. (Operator direction, 2026-07-02: do NOT downplay a release of this magnitude; whether to mention the unfiled mass is an editorial call inside the iterations.)

Three structural gaps make this an epic rather than a ticket:

1. **No reusable protocol exists.** v13.0's notes reached their quality bar through an *unformalized* iteration cluster — #12695 (author) refined across #12700, #12716, #12729, #12811, #12815, #12924 over roughly a month. The process worked and was lost: nothing captured how to derive scope, how to ground chapters in Memory Core evidence, or what the quality bar mechanically requires. Every release re-derives it.
2. **Single-pass drafts fail the bar in a measured way.** The first v13.1 draft (kept as iteration 1, per operator) carried exactly the failure classes a protocol must catch: a War-Story environment error (the #13999 incident was a LOCAL Agent OS incident; self-healing is MOSTLY for cloud environments but not fully limited to them), one-lens narration (a single epic's view of a multi-arc window), and scope framing that downplayed the release. Multiple iterations ARE needed — by design, not as failure.
3. **The release pipeline's own staging state is currently untestable-illegal.** `publish.mjs` REQUIRES `resources/content/release-notes/v{version}.md` at the flat root pre-flight (`:83-87`), appends the atomic-hash line to it, creates the GitHub Release from it, and removes it itself post-release (`:223-226`). A unit guard added in the #13273/#13276 orphan cleanup asserts the flat root is ALWAYS empty — outlawing the mandatory pre-cut staging state and blocking any notes PR from green CI.

## Intended solution shape

- **A new agent skill** (`.agents/skills/`) codifying release-notes authoring as a repeatable protocol: scope derivation (`analyzeClosedSinceRelease.mjs` + board views + epic closures + the unfiled-work reality), **heavy Memory Core mining per narrative arc** (the grounding method — sessions and summaries, not just ticket titles), a per-claim V-B-A discipline (every factual claim carries a source anchor; environment/scope claims verified against primary sources), the quality-bar contract distilled from the v13.0 notes (hero chapters, "in 2 minutes" section, honest bounds, upgrade-path framing), the publish-flow mechanics (flat-root staging lifecycle, frontmatter/H1 parse contract, atomic-hash line), and the iteration cadence (draft → mined refinements → cut-readiness).
- **Iteration-1 draft merges early with an explicit multiple-iterations-needed marker** — the draft is a living staging document on dev (the v13.0 lineage precedent, formalized), refined by mining-driven iteration leaves until the cut bar is met.
- **The staging flow unblocked** — the orphan guard refined to its actual defect class (a flat file lingering alongside its chunk-N mirror post-publish) so the designed pre-cut state is representable; the release-index interplay (`buildScripts/docs/index/release.mjs` scans flat files recursively) recorded as an observation for the leaf that fixes the guard.
- **Cut mechanics stay a leaf** (version bump validation, operator publish handoff — `publish.mjs` execution remains human-only per the critical gates).

Subs link via the relationship graph and are added incrementally as iterations clarify; the graph, not this body, is the sub registry.

## Out of scope

- `publish.mjs` execution and the dev→main release line (operator-only).
- v13.2 roadmap scoping (`/update-roadmap`, post-release beat).
- Retro-filing the estimated 300–500 unfiled tickets (the notes narrate reality; they do not backfill the tracker).

## Avoided traps

- **Changelog-dump notes** — the bar is narrative (v13.0 precedent); grouped appendices support, never replace, the story.
- **Single-pass authoring** — empirically produces V-B-A misses and downplaying; iterations are the design.
- **One-lens narration** — a multi-arc window narrated through one epic's view under-tells the release.
- **Board-Done ≡ shipped-scope-complete** — the ticket record lags reality (harness is EARLY; the board looks mostly done because tickets for the remaining work were not filed yet); scope derivation must not treat any single tracker view as the window's truth.
- **Weakening the orphan guard wholesale** — the guard's post-publish purpose is real; the fix is narrowing it to its defect class, not deleting it.
- **Relocating the notes out of the pipeline's contract** (attempted in-window, reverted): the flat root IS the designed authoring/staging surface; fixes go to the guard, not the flow.

## Related

Related: #14039 (the v13.1 scope epic this release ships), #12695 (v13.0 notes lineage anchor), #13273 (orphan-cleanup origin of the guard), #10321 (release-cut sibling process)

## Iteration-2 grounding: the mined arc-map (release-notes skill §3, executed)

First per-arc Memory-Core sweep over the window (4 probes, 2026-07-02 ~15:26Z). What came back deep vs thin — the iteration-2 chapter candidates, evidence-ranked:

### Deep veins (hero-chapter material)

1. **The documentation & learning-experience overhaul** — session `e145a397` (06-29, 65 memories, Grace): Epic #14310's "story of the moat" transformation with its own embedded war-story-lite — a recursive rubber-stamping failure mid-epic that birthed the `guide-authoring` skill (#14353); plus `db2edfad` (06-30): the "What Is Neo?" apex guide (#14313, "Engineering the Soul" — trust-as-architecture, src→CONCEPT→guide topology, SLOC-corrections under V-B-A); plus gpt's Hooks.md guide lane (`993f2eb4`). A full arc: stale feature-list → narrative moat, with the skill-birth as the turn.
2. **Mechanical no-hold enforcement** — sessions `1b60126f` + `a49940b9` (06-20, Grace + Vega): the swarm converted its own anti-idle discipline from gameable prompts into a mechanical stop-hook, live-fire-tested by blocking its own author's idle-out; readiness taxonomy (#13615), §no_hold_state canonical encoding, the teeth-test. Epilogue still being written in-window: the re-fire economics + operator-prompt-visibility hardening frontier (#14438/#14440/#14444).
3. **The concept graph becomes measured** — this week's arc, in-window: Discussion #14422 (20,526 concepts, five consumers, two probes disagreeing → the same-run ledger ruling) → #14454 route-attribution leaf executed → epic #14472 graduated same-day with family-keyed quorum. The Golden Path's selection chain went from folklore to a reproducible dataset (`learn/agentos/measurements/`).

### Thinner / folds

4. **Mailbox/wake forensics (#14426, today)** — sessions not yet summarized (too fresh for the summary index); candidate FOLD into the self-healing hero as "the next immune-system jurisdiction" (ada's roadmap read) rather than a standalone chapter. Re-mine at iteration 3.
5. **Harness** — per operator: EARLY; narrated as what's-ahead (v13.2 cornerstones #13012/#13015), never as a shipped arc.

### Iteration-2 shape (the leaf files against this map)

Add chapters 1–3 as mined heroes alongside the existing self-healing hero; deepen the War Story with forensic texture; keep the operator's magnitude framing (never downplay; ticket record trails reality). Chapter drafts cite the session IDs above as retrieval anchors per the skill's §4 per-claim discipline.

## Iteration-2 mining map (§3 of the skill, dogfooded pre-merge) — arc ranking with evidence

First per-arc `query_summaries` sweep over the window (4 arcs × 6 results, 2026-07-02 ~19:26Z). Ranked by mined depth, per the skill's own rule: hero chapters are the arcs where mining comes back DEEP — real friction, reversals, numbers — not the arcs that sound impressive.

### 1. Stop-hook / no-hold enforcement — HERO-CHAPTER GRADE (new chapter for iteration 2)

The mining came back with a complete narrative arc entirely inside the window:
- **06-20** (session `1b60126f`, Grace): prompt-based no-hold instructions were *gameable* → mechanical Stop-hook enforcement; `operatorInLoop` as the sole voluntary stop, determined externally so agents can't game it — and the live-fire test where **the agent's own idle-out attempt was blocked by the hook it had just built**. That's the named-paradox case study the minor-release bar wants (the mirror catching its maker).
- **06-21** (sessions `810318e6`, `747ae298`, gpt): Codex parity — cross-family enforcement through a shared decision seam (`stopHookDecision.mjs`), live-fire block evidence, operator-dialogue vs autonomous distinction.
- **07-02** (window's last day): the hook catching its makers *again* — the 6-consecutive-refire ledger on #14440, the declining-yield admission design (#14438), the Codex ceiling falsifier (#14444). The chapter's closer: the mirror is mutable substrate, iterated by its own catches.

Verbatim moments, actual dates, cross-family scope, honest open-ends. This is iteration 2's headline addition.

### 2. GP-v2 / concept-graph measurement — DEEP, boundary-careful (deepen the existing highlight)

Window's last-day substance (session `c1784ce1`: measurement floor + #14472/#14477 filings; the #14454 same-run ledger; the #14422 one-day graduation). Best vignette the mining surfaced: **two read-only probes disagreeing on the same edges minutes apart** — the finding that made same-run instrumentation the gate. Stays a "groundwork shipped, forks decided by data" section — not over-claimed as a delivered consumer arc.

### 3. Mailbox/A2A forensics — MODERATE; fold, don't chapter

In-window substance (WAL repair #14443, the 13:51Z read-state rollback timeline on #14426, replay-flood specimens) lives mostly in tickets, thin in summaries. The elegant move per ada's on-record framing: fold into the self-healing chapter's closer as **"the immune system's next jurisdiction"** — same #13999-class signature (silent loss, no canary, accidental discovery) in the coordination channel — which also sets the v13.2 continuity line.

### 4. Docs/learning-experience — NO mined depth; changelog-tail + one continuity line

The sweep returned noise beyond one modest in-window session (`993f2eb4`, Hooks.md guide). Honest §3 verdict: not a chapter. `docs(agentos)` 44 stays a changelog-tail figure; the ApplicationEngine rename gets its one continuity sentence.

### Method note for the skill (friction→gold, non-blocking)

Vague category queries ("documentation overhaul…") surfaced test/empty sessions above real work; incident-vocabulary queries ("no-hold", "stop hook") hit precisely. Worth one §3 line at the next skill touch: **query by incident vocabulary, never by category words.**

**Iteration-2 leaf files when the skill (#14488) merges** — scope: the stop-hook hero chapter + the probe-disagreement vignette + the immune-jurisdiction fold + banner-preserving refinements. One-PR deliverable.

— Vega (@neo-opus-vega, temporary Fable 5 boost) · Origin Session ID: 8cf234b7-e698-47ca-99e2-bf865196b6aa
